TypeSignature.Value: public interface IQueryable<out T> : System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<out T>, System.Linq.IQueryable

.class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IQueryable`1<+ T> implements class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<!T>, class System.Collections.IEnumerable, class System.Linq.IQueryable
Public Interface IQueryable(Of Out T) Implements IEnumerable(Of Out T), IQueryable
type IQueryable<'T> = interface interface seq<'T> interface IEnumerable interface IQueryable
generic <typename T> public interface class IQueryable : System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<T>, System::Linq::IQueryable

Docs.typeparam: The type of the data in the data source.
Docs.summary: Provides functionality to evaluate queries against a specific data source wherein the type of the data is known.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable`1> interface is intended for implementation by query providers.
This interface inherits the <xref:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1> interface so that if it represents a query, the results of that query can be enumerated. Enumeration forces the expression tree associated with an <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable`1> object to be executed. Queries that do not return enumerable results are executed when the <xref:System.Linq.IQueryProvider.Execute``1(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression)> method is called.
The definition of "executing an expression tree" is specific to a query provider. For example, it may involve translating the expression tree to a query language appropriate for an underlying data source.
The <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable`1> interface enables queries to be polymorphic. That is, because a query against an `IQueryable` data source is represented as an expression tree, it can be executed against different types of data sources.
The `static` (`Shared` in Visual Basic) methods defined in the class <xref:System.Linq.Queryable> (except for <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.AsQueryable*>, <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenBy*>, and <xref:System.Linq.Queryable.ThenByDescending*>) extend objects of types that implement the <xref:System.Linq.IQueryable`1> interface.
For more information about how to create your own LINQ provider, see [LINQ: Building an IQueryable Provider](https://learn.microsoft.com/archive/blogs/mattwar/linq-building-an-iqueryable-provider-part-i).
